rulings.Case Title: LEA HYKE VS. WOODWORKS WINDOW & DOOR COMPANY INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 8, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF LEA HYKE's Motion For Assignment Order. Granted. No opposition filed. The following rights to payment of Judgment Debtor, Woodworks Window and Door Company, are hereby assigned to the judgment creditor, Lea Hyke, until such time as the judgment herein is fully satisfied or this order is amended: accounts receivable/payments due from (1) Bryan Michelson Construction; (2) Scott A. Meadows; (3) Pacific Ridge Construction; (4) Justin Farrelly, Inc.; and (5) McMullen Brothers Inc. The aforementioned third parties shall pay the subject accounts receivables/payments to judgment creditor Lea Hyke c/o Sweet & Walker, P.C., 6073 Mission Street, Daly City, California 94014, to be applied to the judgment herein until such judgment is fully satisfied or this order is amended. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MATS NAHLINDER VS. MARKMONITOR, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Markmonitor, Incorporated'S Motion To Re-Open The Deposition Of Pltf Mats Nahlinder; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 8, 2013, Line 13, Defendant MarkMonitor, Incorporated's Motion To Re-Open The Deposition Of Plaintiff Mats Nahlinder; Request For Sanctions. Granted for good cause shown. Subsequent discovery has revealed that the document production made at the time of Plaintiff's deposition did not include all responsive documents and information that were reasonably available or known at the time of the original deposition. Plaintiff's deposition will be reopened for the limited purpose of questioning Plaintiff on emails in his supplemental production, his failure to produce the emails in response to Defendant's October 2011 discovery requests, any other MarkMonitor documents in his possession and any issues arising from these topics. Monetary sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: LITWAK & ASSOCIATES, P.C., A CALIFORNIA et al VS. NICHOLAS K EVANS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One To Deft
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 8, 2013, Line 3: PLAINTIFFS LITWAK & ASSOCIATES, P.C.'S and MARTIN LITWAK'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One To Defendant Pro Tem Judge Weixel, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: To the extent that Plaintiffs seek to compel production, the motion is GRANTED, with production to take place within 20 days of this order. Although Plaintiffs' motion is phrased as a motion to compel further responses rather than to compel production, the Court construes the motion as a request for the latter as well. The parties are reminded that the two are distinct. (See Code Civ. Proc., § 2031.310 (re motions to compel further responses to requests for production); § 2031.320 (re motions to compel compliance).) The difference can be significant, as different timelines apply. (Cf. §§ 2031.310, subd. (c) (45-day time limit for filing motion to compel further responses); § 2031.320 (no time limit for filing motion to compel compliance.) That difference comes into play to the extent that Plaintiffs seek to compel further responses to Requests Nos. 1-24, 26-31, 33-35, 39-45, and 49-51. The Court believes Plaintiffs' motion as to those requests has substantive merit, but is more inclined to find that it was untimely. Therefore, the parties are ordered to address the issue of timeliness at the hearing, specifically whether counsel's January 22, 2013 email colloquy established a deadline of March 1, 2013 for filing a motion to compel. The issue of sanctions is reserved for hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to appeals@jimweixel.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LITWAK & ASSOCIATES, P.C., A CALIFORNIA et al VS. NICHOLAS K EVANS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Demand For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 8, 2013, Line 2: CROSS COMPLAINANT NICHOLAS EVANS' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Demand For Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge Weixel, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED. Moving party did not file a separate statement in support of the motion. (Cal. Rules of Court, rule 3.1345(a) (stating that moving party "must provide" a separate statement).) The failure to file a separate statement is a sufficient basis upon which to deny the motion. (Cf. Mills v. US Bank (2008) 166 Cal.App.4th 871, 893 (affirming denial of motion because moving party had filed a nonconforming separate statement).) Moving party did not file any separate statement, let alone a nonconforming one. And although it may be true that the opposition papers were filed a day late, that does not change the fact that moving party did not fulfill the mandatory prerequisite of filing a separate statement. Therefore, the motion is denied. The issue of sanctions is reserved for hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to appeals@jimweixel.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN V ANDREWS et al VS. TETRA TECH EC, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Compelling Further Responses And Production To Inspection Demands To Ptlf'S First Demand For The Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 8, 2013, Line 3: PLAINTIFFS SUSAN ANDREWS, ARTHUR JAHR, ELBERT BOWERS, and ARCHIE JACKSON'S Motion Compelling Further Responses And Production To Inspection Demands To Plaintiff's First Demand For The Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge Weixel, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: To the extent that Plaintiffs seek to compel production, the motion is GRANTED as to Requests Nos. 1, 2, 3, 5, 7, 8, 9, 11, and 12; GRANTED as to Requests No. 15 and 16 to the extent limited by Plaintiff in meet-and-confer efforts; GRANTED as to Requests Nos. 4 and 6, but production is limited to documents pertaining to radiological remediation personnel; DENIED as duplicative as to Request No. 13; and DENIED as to Request No. 14. As to No. 14, even if counsel's notes are subject only to a qualified privilege under the attorney work product doctrine, the same information can be obtained from Catherine Daly, and therefore Plaintiff has not shown that her case will be unfairly prejudiced or an injustice will result if counsel's notes are not produced. (Code Civ. Proc., § 2018.030; Coito v. Superior Court (2012) 54 Cal.4th 480, 496-497.) Defendant's objections for vagueness, ambiguity, and proprietary or confidential information are OVERRULED. Defendant shall serve further responses as ordered and produce documents within 20 days of the date of this order. As to each document for which a privilege is claimed, Defendant shall provide a privilege log with sufficient information to establish the factual and legal foundation for the privilege. (Code Civ. Proc., § 2031.240(c).) The issue of sanctions is reserved for hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to appeals@jimweixel.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LINDA DAVIDSON VS. MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 8, 2013, Line 9, DEFENDANT MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained in part and overruled in part. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for causes of action 4, 5, and 6. (Plaintiff states in opposition that she intends to amend the 4th and 5th causes of action.) The demurrer is overruled for cause of action 7. The CLRA claim (cause of action 5) is sustained with leave to amend to plead service of the notice in the complaint and to plead misrepresentation more clearly in the complaint. The UCL claim (cause of action 6) is sustained with leave to amend to plead particular facts regarding how Defendant's conduct was unfair, unlawful, or fraudulent. Plaintiff must plead facts beyond restating the breach of contract. The Negligence claim (cause of action 7) is overruled. Plaintiff has alleged all the elements required for negligence. Defendant's request for judicial notice of Plaintiff's complaint is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
